title: Update working memory from session
summary: Durable context learned during the current coding session about active playlist refresh placement and Xtream account-info styling.

claims.text: WorkspaceShellHeaderComponent exposes a dedicated refresh-playlist action: canRefreshPlaylist and isRefreshingPlaylist inputs control a sync icon button, and refreshPlaylistRequested emits when it is clicked.

WorkspaceShellFacade computes canRefreshPlaylist and isRefreshingPlaylist from PlaylistRefreshActionService for the active playlist, and refreshCurrentPlaylist delegates the active playlist to that service.

PlaylistRefreshActionService only marks a playlist refreshable when window.electron is present and the playlist has serverUrl, url, or filePath; it routes Xtream refresh through confirmation, content deletion, restore-data persistence, metadata update, and workspace navigation, while M3U refresh uses PlaylistRefreshService and dispatches an NgRx playlist update.

The session identified that, before adding the active-playlist header action, M3U and Xtream refresh or reimport was reachable from the sources list, which forced users already browsing a playlist to navigate away to refresh stale content.

Playlist refresh should not be placed in the left navigation rail because the rail is a navigation surface, not an action bar, and a context-dependent refresh control there would make the rail semantics inconsistent.

Active playlist refresh uses a separate dedicated workspace-header action instead of overloading headerShortcut because headerShortcut is a single-action slot already used by the M3U video player for Open Multi EPG, and refresh needs to coexist with that shortcut.

Adding refresh to the playlist switcher's context-actions-section was discussed as a secondary discovery path, but this session deliberately left that placement unbuilt when the user chose the primary header option.

The Xtream account info dialog SCSS follows the app widget and provider theme tokens for its hero, panels, provider accent, shadows, and surfaces instead of a standalone hardcoded gradient-heavy style.

sources.title: SWE-chat Claude Code session: Xtream account info design cleanup

metadata.reason: The user asked where to add refresh because it was only available from the sources list, and the session analysis recorded that as the pre-existing friction.
The brainstorm explicitly evaluated the navigation rail option and rejected it because it would mix actions into a navigation surface.
During implementation the session found headerShortcut was a single-action slot already used for Open Multi EPG and chose a dedicated refresh action so both controls can coexist.
The brainstorm recommended the playlist switcher as a secondary placement, but the user approved only the primary header option for implementation.
